3. **Restart the daemon:** `just stop && just up` — `panopt up` reuses a running daemon, so a code change needs a fresh process.
4. **MCP smoke from an agent:** in a Claude Code session pointed at panopt, exercise:
   - `mcp__panopt__todo_search {}` — should return the same set as `todo_list`.
   - `mcp__panopt__todo_search {"query": "search"}` — should include todo #33 (title `search todos`).
   - `mcp__panopt__todo_search {"status": "open", "priority": "high"}` — sanity-check the filter combination.
   - `mcp__panopt__todo_search {"tags": ["nonexistent"]}` — should return `[]`.
   - `mcp__panopt__scratchpad_search {"query": "<a known substring>"}` — confirm body matching works.
   - `mcp__panopt__todo_search {"status": "bogus"}` — should return an `invalid_params` error, matching `todo_update`'s behavior for bad enum input.
5. **Projection unaffected:** confirm `.panopt/todos.md` and `.panopt/scratchpads.md` are untouched after a search (search is read-only — there should be no projection writes triggered).

toolUseResult.plan:
# Plan: Search todos and scratchpads (todo #33)

## Context

Todo #33 ("search todos") asks for a way to find todos — and per its body, "probably scratchpads, too" — by text and filters. Today both `todo_list` and `scratchpad_list` are no-arg fetch-everything tools, so any agent wanting "all open bug-tagged todos" has to pull the full table and filter client-side. As the project accumulates todos and scratchpads this becomes wasteful for agents and unworkable as a UX (e.g. the cockpit can't lazily query, it has to ship the whole list).

This plan adds two new MCP tools, `todo_search` and `scratchpad_search`, returning the same summary DTOs as the existing list tools but accepting optional text + structured filters. Sibling todo #46 ("universal object search") is the eventual cross-kind search; this MVP is the per-kind precursor and intentionally does not unify the two kinds yet.

**Approach: parameterized SQL with `LIKE` for text matching, exact-match for enums/assignee, tags filtered in Rust after fetch.** No FTS5, no schema migration. Rationale: the project scale (typically <1000 todos/scratchpads per project) makes `LIKE` plenty fast, and adding the `fts5` rusqlite feature plus a virtual-table migration is a meaningful surface area that should wait until search performance actually bites. FTS5 stays a clean future swap because the tool signatures don't expose how matching is implemented.

## Tool surface

Two new tools with optional fields throughout — an all-omitted call is equivalent to the existing `*_list` (so the search tool subsumes list for callers willing to pay one extra arg).

```rust
// panopt-tool-surface/src/params.rs
#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema)]
pub struct TodoSearchArgs {
    /// Case-insensitive substring matched against title and body. Omit to
    /// match all todos (subject to other filters).
    #[serde(default)]
    pub query: Option<String>,
    /// Restrict to this status. One of open/in_progress/backlog/draft/completed/not_done.
    #[serde(default)]
    pub status: Option<String>,
    /// Restrict to this priority. One of high/medium/low.
    #[serde(default)]
    pub priority: Option<String>,
    /// Case-insensitive exact match on assignee name.
    #[serde(default)]
    pub assignee: Option<String>,
    /// Require every listed tag to be present on the todo (AND semantics).
    #[serde(default)]
    pub tags: Option<Vec<String>>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, JsonSchema)]
pub struct ScratchpadSearchArgs {
    /// Case-insensitive substring matched against title and body.
    #[serde(default)]
    pub query: Option<String>,
    /// Require every listed tag to be present (AND semantics).
    #[serde(default)]
    pub tags: Option<Vec<String>>,
}
```

Returns: `Vec<TodoSummaryDto>` / `Vec<ScratchpadDto>` — identical shape to `todo_list`/`scratchpad_list`, so existing consumers can swap in.

## Concrete changes

### 1. `crates/panopt-tool-surface/src/params.rs`
Append the two structs above. Match the doc-comment voice of `TodoUpdateArgs` (`params.rs:107`) and `ScratchpadUpdateArgs` (`params.rs:43`).

### 2. `crates/panopt-tool-surface/src/lib.rs`
Add two `ToolDef` entries to `TOOL_SURFACE`. Place `scratchpad_search` immediately after `scratchpad_list` (`lib.rs:122`) and `todo_search` immediately after `todo_list` (`lib.rs:169`), to keep the alphabetical-ish locality the file uses.

```rust
ToolDef {
    name: "todo_search",
    description: "Find todos. Optional `query` substring-matches title and body \
                  (case-insensitive); optional status/priority/assignee/tags \
                  filters narrow further (tags AND together). Returns the same \
                  shape as todo_list. Call with no args for an effective alias \
                  of todo_list.",
    schema_fn: schema_for::<TodoSearchArgs>,
},
ToolDef {
    name: "scratchpad_search",
    description: "Find scratchpads. Optional `query` substring-matches title \
                  and body (case-insensitive); optional tags filter ANDs \
                  together. Returns the same shape as scratchpad_list.",
    schema_fn: schema_for::<ScratchpadSearchArgs>,
},
```

### 3. `crates/panopt-core/src/state.rs`
Add two query functions next to the existing list functions.

- `scratchpad_search` near `scratchpad_list` at `state.rs:353`.
- `todo_search` near `todo_list` at `state.rs:890`.

Pattern — match the prepare → query_map → collect style already in place:

```rust
pub fn todo_search(
    &self,
    project: ProjectId,
    query: Option<&str>,
    status: Option<TodoStatus>,
    priority: Option<Priority>,
    assignee: Option<&str>,
    require_tags: &[String],
) -> Result<Vec<Todo>, CoreError> {
    // Build a parameterized WHERE clause. Push `query`, `status`, `priority`,
    // `assignee` (LOWER(...) = LOWER(?)) into SQL. Collect candidate ids,
    // hydrate via fetch_todo (matches todo_list's two-phase pattern), then
    // filter on require_tags in Rust against the hydrated Vec<String> tags.
    // Always preserves `deleted_at IS NULL` and `ORDER BY id`.
}
```

Key SQL fragments:
- Text match: `(LOWER(title) LIKE ?1 OR LOWER(body) LIKE ?1)` with the bound value `format!("%{}%", query.to_lowercase())`.
- Status/priority: store as their textual representations matching how `todo_update` writes them; reuse `parse_status` / `parse_priority` in the handler to convert string → enum before calling the state method.
- Tags: keep filtering in Rust after hydration, mirroring `tags_list` at `state.rs:1189` which already pays the JSON-parse cost. AND semantics: `require_tags.iter().all(|t| todo.tags.iter().any(|x| x == t))`.

For `scratchpad_search`, return `Vec<(u64, String)>` to mirror `scratchpad_list`. Since the (id, title) shape doesn't carry body or tags, the function needs to fetch (id, title, body, tags) for filtering, then project back to (id, title) on output:

```rust
pub fn scratchpad_search(
    &self,
    project: ProjectId,
    query: Option<&str>,
    require_tags: &[String],
) -> Result<Vec<(u64, String)>, CoreError> {
    // SELECT id, title, body, tags ... WHERE deleted_at IS NULL
    // and LOWER(title)/LOWER(body) LIKE ?  (only when query is Some)
    // Then in Rust: parse tags JSON, keep rows where every require_tags entry
    // is present. Project to (id, title).
}
```

### 4. `crates/panoptd/src/handler.rs`
Four edits — `Tool` enum, `Tool::from_name`, `dispatch_local` match, plus the two new handler methods.

- Add `TodoSearch` after `TodoList` in the enum at `handler.rs:1283`.
- Add `ScratchpadSearch` after `ScratchpadList` at `handler.rs:1275`.
- Add the two arms in `Tool::from_name` (`handler.rs:1311`):
  ```rust
  "todo_search" => Tool::TodoSearch,
  "scratchpad_search" => Tool::ScratchpadSearch,
  ```
- Add dispatch arms in `dispatch_local` (`handler.rs:1410`), matching the args-parsing pattern of `Tool::TodoGet` / `Tool::ScratchpadGet`.
- Add `todo_search` method modeled on `todo_list` (`handler.rs:814`), mapping each hydrated `Todo` via `TodoSummaryDto::from_todo`. Parse `status`/`priority` strings up front using the existing `parse_status` / `parse_priority` helpers (already used by `todo_update` at `handler.rs:843`) so callers get `invalid_params` errors for bad enum values, consistent with `todo_update`.
- Add `scratchpad_search` method modeled on `scratchpad_list` (`handler.rs:702`), wrapping `(id, title)` pairs into `ScratchpadDto`.

### 5. `crates/panoptd/src/handler.rs` — coverage test
The `tool_enum_covers_tool_surface` test at `handler.rs:1648` already iterates `TOOL_SURFACE` and asserts each resolves through `Tool::from_name`. Adding the two `TOOL_SURFACE` entries plus the two `from_name` arms makes the test pass; the exhaustive `match Tool` in `dispatch_local` makes the dispatch arm a compile error rather than a runtime miss. No test code edit needed beyond running it.

## Critical files

- `crates/panopt-tool-surface/src/params.rs` — add `TodoSearchArgs`, `ScratchpadSearchArgs`
- `crates/panopt-tool-surface/src/lib.rs` — add two `TOOL_SURFACE` entries (around lines 125 and 175)
- `crates/panopt-core/src/state.rs` — add `todo_search` (near :890), `scratchpad_search` (near :353)
- `crates/panoptd/src/handler.rs` — `Tool` variants (around :1275/:1283), `from_name` arms (around :1322/:1331), `dispatch_local` arms (around :1431/:1457), new methods modeled on `todo_list`/`scratchpad_list` (around :702/:814)

## Reuse / existing patterns

- `TodoSummaryDto::from_todo` and `ScratchpadDto` — already define output shape; do not invent new DTOs.
- `parse_status`, `parse_priority` (used at `handler.rs:843`) — reuse for string→enum.
- `enter(&mut st, &parts)` + `state.lock()` + `map_core_err` — uniform handler scaffolding; copy from `todo_list`.
- `fetch_todo` (called by `todo_list`) — reuse for hydration in `todo_search`, so blockers/comments aggregation stays in one place.
- Soft-delete predicate `deleted_at IS NULL` — already canonical, included in every list path.

## Out of scope

- FTS5 / ranking — `LIKE` is the MVP; document a follow-up todo if results are unranked enough to bother agents in practice.
- Cross-kind universal search (todo #46) — separate effort; this plan keeps the per-kind seams clean so a future universal tool can call both.
- Searching comment bodies — todo comments are a separate table; not part of the user's stated need.
- Bidirectional `.panopt/*.md` reads — projection stays write-only.
- Schema migration — none required.

## Verification

1. **Compile sweep:** `just check && just clippy` — exercises the workspace and the WASM plugin. The exhaustive `match Tool` in `dispatch_local` will refuse to compile if either dispatch arm is missing.
2. **Coverage test:** `cargo test -p panoptd tool_enum_covers_tool_surface` — confirms `todo_search` and `scratchpad_search` are wired through `Tool::from_name`.
3. **Restart the daemon:** `just stop && just up` — `panopt up` reuses a running daemon, so a code change needs a fresh process.
4. **MCP smoke from an agent:** in a Claude Code session pointed at panopt, exercise:
   - `mcp__panopt__todo_search {}` — should return the same set as `todo_list`.
   - `mcp__panopt__todo_search {"query": "search"}` — should include todo #33 (title `search todos`).
   - `mcp__panopt__todo_search {"status": "open", "priority": "high"}` — sanity-check the filter combination.
   - `mcp__panopt__todo_search {"tags": ["nonexistent"]}` — should return `[]`.
   - `mcp__panopt__scratchpad_search {"query": "<a known substring>"}` — confirm body matching works.
   - `mcp__panopt__todo_search {"status": "bogus"}` — should return an `invalid_params` error, matching `todo_update`'s behavior for bad enum input.
5. **Projection unaffected:** confirm `.panopt/todos.md` and `.panopt/scratchpads.md` are untouched after a search (search is read-only — there should be no projection writes triggered).

50	/// `Backlog` is not yet scheduled, `Open` is ready to pick up,
51	/// `InProgress` is being worked, `Completed` is done, and `NotDone`
52	/// records that the todo was closed without being done (cancelled,
53	/// won't-fix, superseded). Both `Completed` and `NotDone` are terminal;
54	/// only `Completed` sets `completed_at`.
55	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
56	pub enum TodoStatus {
57	    #[default]
58	    Open,
59	    InProgress,
60	    Backlog,
61	    Draft,
62	    Completed,
63	    NotDone,
64	}
65	
66	impl TodoStatus {
67	    /// The token stored in SQLite and used on the wire and in the projection.
68	    pub fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
69	        match self {
70	            TodoStatus::Open => "open",
71	            TodoStatus::InProgress => "in_progress",
72	            TodoStatus::Backlog => "backlog",
73	            TodoStatus::Draft => "draft",
74	            TodoStatus::Completed => "completed",
75	            TodoStatus::NotDone => "not_done",
76	        }
77	    }
78	
79	    /// Parse a stored or wire token; `None` for an unrecognized string.
80	    pub fn parse(s: &str) -> Option<TodoStatus> {
81	        match s {
82	            "open" => Some(TodoStatus::Open),
83	            "in_progress" => Some(TodoStatus::InProgress),
84	            "backlog" => Some(TodoStatus::Backlog),
85	            "draft" => Some(TodoStatus::Draft),
86	            "completed" => Some(TodoStatus::Completed),
87	            "not_done" => Some(TodoStatus::NotDone),
88	            _ => None,
89	        }
90	    }
91	}

content.content:
1	//! The single declaration of panopt's MCP tool surface.
2	//!
3	//! `panoptd` and `panopt _mcp-proxy` are both MCP servers that must publish
4	//! identical `tools/list` output. To prevent drift, this crate holds the one
5	//! source of truth: the `Parameters<T>` types whose `JsonSchema` derives
6	//! produce each tool's input schema (under [`params`]), and [`TOOL_SURFACE`]
7	//! which names every tool with its description and schema-generating
8	//! function.
9	//!
10	//! Both binaries iterate [`TOOL_SURFACE`] at startup to register their tool
11	//! routes - panoptd's routes call the real impls, the proxy's routes forward
12	//! to panoptd. There is no other declaration of the tool list anywhere;
13	//! adding a tool means adding one [`ToolDef`] here, one impl in panoptd's
14	//! dispatcher, and nothing in the proxy.
15	
16	use schemars::generate::SchemaSettings;
17	use schemars::JsonSchema;
18	
19	pub mod params;
20	
21	// Re-export schemars so consumers don't pick up a second schemars version by
22	// accident. During the macro->data-driven migration (steps 2-4 of todo #88)
23	// panoptd's `#[tool]` macros need a `JsonSchema` impl that lines up with
24	// rmcp's pinned schemars; sharing this one re-export keeps the two in step.
25	pub use schemars;
26	
27	use crate::params::*;
28	
29	/// One MCP tool's publication metadata.
30	///
31	/// `schema_fn` rather than a baked schema so each entry can reference its
32	/// concrete `Parameters<T>` type without a const-eval dance: at registration
33	/// time the consumer calls the function pointer to get the schema for that
34	/// tool's arguments.
35	pub struct ToolDef {
36	    pub name: &'static str,
37	    pub description: &'static str,
38	    pub schema_fn: fn() -> serde_json::Value,
39	}
40	
41	/// Generate a tool's input schema from its argument type.
42	///
43	/// Matches `rmcp::handler::server::common::schema_for_type` byte-for-byte:
44	/// a draft2020-12 `SchemaSettings` generator, root-schema-for-`T`, serialized
45	/// to a JSON object. Used in [`TOOL_SURFACE`] entries as
46	/// `schema_fn: schema_for::<T>` so each entry is a plain function pointer.
47	/// Bit-identical output between this crate and rmcp matters so that the
48	/// step-4 drift assertion (and Claude's tool-list cache invalidation) treat
49	/// the two as truly the same schema.
50	pub fn schema_for<T: JsonSchema>() -> serde_json::Value {
51	    let generator = SchemaSettings::draft2020_12().into_generator();
52	    let schema = generator.into_root_schema_for::<T>();
53	    serde_json::to_value(schema).expect("a JsonSchema-derived schema always serializes to JSON")
54	}
55	
56	/// Empty-object schema used for tools that take no arguments.

attachment.content:
- verify-all: Run the full check + clippy sweep across the workspace AND the WASM plugin (panopt-zellij). Use after non-trivial edits, before declaring work done, or when asked to verify the repo builds cleanly. Captures the dual-target dance (workspace builds for host, panopt-zellij builds only for wasm32-wasip1) so you don't have to remember the right manifest-path/target each time.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request

content.text:
Quick context on what exists: the Zellij sidebar already has a `TodoFilter` (status-based — `OpenUnblocked`, `All`, etc.) shown in the title bar as `[OpenUnblocked]`, with key handling and a parsed `(id, label)` list per pane. Crucially the plugin reads `.panopt/todos.md` — it's a projection consumer, not an MCP client. That shapes the design.

**The architectural fork**

- **Local filter** — match against the already-parsed labels in `self.items`. Free, instant, no new infra. But the projection only contains title + status, so **no body search** — only title substring works.
- **MCP-backed** — call the new `todo_search` from the plugin. Body matching, full filter set. But the WASM plugin doesn't talk HTTP to the daemon today (no bearer token loading, no rmcp client in wasm). That's real new surface for a feature whose 90% case is "find todo by title".

I'd start local. Body search is a Claude-side concern; the sidebar is for navigation.

**UX: `/` over "custom filter"**

`/` is the right entry point — vim/less/fzf muscle memory, transient by default. The "custom filter" framing (cycling `[Search:foo]` alongside `[OpenUnblocked]`) adds friction: you have to know how to set it and clear it.

But the two ideas aren't exclusive — make `/` populate a search predicate that *composes* with `TodoFilter`. The title bar grows a second segment:

```
panopt todos [OpenUnblocked] [/term] 12/47
```

Press `/`, type live, results narrow as you type. Esc clears search (but keeps the status filter). Enter dismisses the prompt overlay but keeps the predicate active — same persistence as `TodoFilter`. `Ctrl+G`/`Ctrl+L` (or just `Esc Esc`) clears both.

**Scope**

`/` should work in **every** pane that has `items: Vec<Item>` — todos, scratchpads, agents, processes. It's a generic predicate over the label string and costs almost nothing to make universal. Doing it just-for-todos would be a regret.

**Two open calls for you**

1. Live-filter-as-you-type vs commit-on-Enter? Live is more responsive and matches fzf; commit-on-Enter is simpler input handling. I'd say live.
2. Match strategy — plain case-insensitive substring (cheap, predictable), or smart-case + space-separated AND tokens (`open auth` matches both)? I'd ship substring and revisit if you ever wish it were smarter.

Want me to plan it that way (local, `/` overlay, universal across panes, substring AND status filter)?

- verify-all: Run the full check + clippy sweep across the workspace AND the WASM plugin (panopt-zellij). Use after non-trivial edits, before declaring work done, or when asked to verify the repo builds cleanly. Captures the dual-target dance (workspace builds for host, panopt-zellij builds only for wasm32-wasip1) so you don't have to remember the right manifest-path/target each time.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Yes — and honestly it's probably the *right* home for the MCP-backed search I just built. The inline `/` is best at "narrow what I'm already looking at" (one kind, title-only, no daemon round-trip); the search MCP tools cover body, tags, status, priority — overkill for an inline filter, but exactly the surface a dedicated pane wants.

**The two roles**

| | Inline `/` | Dedicated search pane |
|---|---|---|
| Scope | One pane | Cross-kind (todos + scratchpads, eventually #46) |
| Matches | Title only (from `.md` projection) | Title + body + tags + status/priority (via MCP) |
| Backend | Local, free | Calls `todo_search` / `scratchpad_search` |
| Disrupts context? | No | Yes (steals focus) |
| Use case | "narrow this list" | "find a thing, anywhere" |

**My take:** they're complementary, but if you only do one, do the **popup**. It's where the heavy filters live, it's the natural home for #46's universal search, and the MCP backend is already there. Add `/` later if scrolling-and-narrowing becomes a frequent enough motion to deserve its own gesture.

Mechanics for the popup, if you go that way: keybind opens a new Zellij pane (same mechanism that spawns Todos/Scratchpads/etc., new `Mode::Search`), top line is a query input, body shows results grouped by kind with a single-letter prefix (`T#33`, `S#90`). Enter on a result *swaps the right-side pane* to that item (same swap-in-place model the rest of the cockpit uses) and closes search. Esc closes without selecting. Live results as you type (debounced ~150ms so we don't hammer the daemon mid-keystroke).

Want me to plan that?

content.text: Launching exploration to map out the plugin architecture and the pane-spawning mechanics.

input.prompt:
I'm designing a dedicated "search popup" plugin pane for panopt's Zellij sidebar. The pane will let the user type a query, fire `todo_search` / `scratchpad_search` MCP tools against the panopt daemon, and show results. I need a complete map of the existing Zellij plugin's internal architecture so I can fit the new Mode in without rewriting plumbing.

The plugin lives in `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs` — a single-file WASM plugin that registers `EventType::Key` and renders text. Five plugin instances run, each in a different `Mode` (Todos, Scratchpads, Agents, Processes, Terminals). Each pane reads `.panopt/*.md` projection files from disk.

Investigate and report:

1. **Mode enum and dispatch.** Show the full `Mode` enum, how `Mode` is set from plugin config (`mode=todos`), and where `self.mode` branches in `handle_key` and `render`.

2. **State struct.** Show the `PanoptPane` struct (around line 313) with every field and a one-line comment on what it holds. I need to know what's per-pane state vs derived per render.

3. **Key handling.** Show `handle_key` in full. Map every key currently handled (up/down/enter/filter cycling/etc.) and how each pane decides which keys are relevant. Specifically: is there any existing pattern for *text input* (typing characters into a buffer), or is everything single-key navigation?

4. **Render pipeline.** Show `render` in full or at least its outer skeleton. How does it draw the title bar, the item list, the cursor/highlight, the bottom indicator? Where is `\x1b` ANSI used? Is there a cursor-positioning convention I should follow?

5. **Item filtering and selection.** How does `self.items: Vec<Item>` get built (presumably from `self.todos` + the active `TodoFilter`)? How is the selected index tracked? On Enter, what happens — does it spawn a swap-in-place pane, send a command, what?

6. **Talking to the daemon from the plugin.** Crucially: does the plugin already call panopt MCP tools or HTTP endpoints from inside the WASM sandbox? Search for `tool_call`, `http`, `reqwest`, `rmcp`, `http_request`, `web_request`, Zellij's `web_request` / `pipe_message_to_plugin`, etc. If the plugin has NO HTTP path today, note that — it changes the design substantially. What CAN the plugin do (read files, run commands, pipe messages)?

7. **Plugin reload / refresh trigger.** What triggers re-reading `.panopt/*.md`? FileSystemEvents? A timer? Show that path.

8. **Bottom indicator / cursor row.** I'll need a query input line — does the existing render leave any "chrome" row reserved for status, or is the whole inner area item rows?

1. How the cockpit currently spawns plugin panes (the swap-in-place model)
2. How global keybinds route to "open this plugin pane" actions
3. How the panopt MCP proxy is launched / authenticated (if relevant — the new pane may need to call MCP)

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have a complete picture. Let me compile the final report:

## Architecture Map: panopt-Zellij Plugin

**File:** `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs` (3008 lines)

---

### 1. Mode Enum and Dispatch
**Lines 62–104:** `Mode` enum with 5 variants (Todos, Agents, Terminals, Commands, Scratchpads). Parsing from config `mode=todos` happens in `load()` at lines 525–538. Each instance initializes with a single mode; no in-pane switching.

**Dispatch points:**
- `handle_key()` line 1404: Matches `self.mode` for mode-specific keys (f/F filter Todos only; n new-item routes by mode; u/d/x mode-aware; 1/2/@/! sort Todos only).
- `rebuild_items()` line 1264: Large match on `self.mode` to populate `self.items` from projected data or pane manifest (lines 1265–1335).
- `update()` line 580: Router for Event types; pipes gated to Todos mode only (line 643).

---

### 2. State Struct `PanoptPane`
**Lines 312–456.** Key fields:

- **Mode & config:** `mode`, `mode_known`, `ws` (project root), `panopt_bin`, `port` (daemon), `permitted`.
- **Projection data (per-pane reloaded every timer tick):** `todos: Vec<(u64, String)>`, `scratchpads`, `processes: Vec<ProcessRow>`, `panes: Vec<PaneRow>`. Built by `reload_data()` (line 988) from `.panopt/*.md` files.
- **Derived per-render:** `items: Vec<Item>` (rebuilt in `rebuild_items()` from above + filter/sort). Not persisted across render calls.
- **UI state (persistent across renders):** `cursor`, `scroll` (index into items list), `last_rows`, `last_cols` (pane geometry).
- **Filter/sort (Todos only):** `todo_filter`, `todo_sort_1`, `todo_sort_2` (cycled by keys, held in memory across rebuilds so user keeps view as projection refreshes).
- **Pane routing:** `plugin_pane` (this pane's id), `slot_pane` (the content pane to swap into), `next_viewer_slot` (counter for routing files).
- **Chrome:** `show_help` (bool; ? toggles help overlay), `last_frame_title`, `initial_preview_done`.

---

### 3. Key Handling
**Lines 1404–1489** (`handle_key`). Patterns:

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| Up/Down/PgUp/PgDn/Home/End | `move_cursor()` + preview; no text input anywhere |
| Enter | `activate_cursor()` → opens document or routes pane (focus moves to content) |
| e (Todos) | `edit_focused_todo()` → open todo doc with focus |
| n (mode-specific) | New todo/scratchpad/agent depending on mode |
| x | `delete_focused()` → spawns delete-gate dialog or closes pane |
| u | `start_focused()` → launch process or focus existing pane |
| d | `stop_focused()` → close pane |
| f / F (Todos) | `cycle_todo_filter()` forward/back |
| 1 / ! / 2 / @ (Todos) | `cycle_todo_sort()` on level 1 or 2 |
| ? | Toggle `self.show_help` (replaces list with help text) |
| / (reserved) | No-op stub |

**Text input:** None. Every action is single-key navigation. `/` is commented as "reserved for future filter behavior" (line 1483).

---

### 4. Render Pipeline
**Lines 692–764** (`render`). Structure:

1. **Setup:** Cache `last_rows`, `last_cols`; sync frame title.
2. **Clear:** ANSI `\x1b[3J` (clear scrollback) + loop `\x1b[{row};1H\x1b[2K` for every body row (lines 723–727).
3. **Body:**
   - If `show_help` is true: print help lines via `help_lines()` (lines 728–736).
   - Else if `items.is_empty()`: print "(none)" dim text at row 1 (line 739).
   - Else: render items from `self.scroll` to `self.scroll + visible` (lines 741–752). Each row gets `\x1b[{row};1H{painted_line}` with cursor positioning. Highlight (inverse video) applied to `focused == (idx == self.cursor)` (line 750).
4. **Status line (Todos only):** Row `body_rows` reserved by `list_rows()` (returns `rows - 1` for Todos, `rows` otherwise; line 1536). Draws sort axes at bottom if mode==Todos and space available (lines 756–762).

**ANSI codes used:**
- `\x1b[3J` — clear scrollback
- `\x1b[{row};1H` — position cursor (row, column 1)
- `\x1b[2K` — clear line
- Inverse/bold applied by `paint()` helper for focused items

**Chrome reservation:** Todos pane reserves 1 row (the last one) for status line showing sort values. Other modes use all body rows for items. Frame title (set via `rename_plugin_pane`) holds mode label + filter/scroll counts + gate refusal.

---

### 5. Item Filtering & Selection
**Lines 1264–1338** (`rebuild_items`). Flow:

- `self.todos` / `self.scratchpads` / `self.processes` / `self.panes` read from disk (line 988).
- **Todos mode:** Filter by `self.todo_filter.includes_label()` (line 1270), then stable two-pass sort: sort-2 then sort-1 (lines 1274–1275). Result: `Vec<Item>` with label `#id title`, target `ItemTarget::Todo(id)`, live=false.
- **Other modes:** Map panes/processes directly to items with live flags (from `pane_active()`, focused state).
- **Selection:** Cursor tracked as `self.cursor: usize` in `0..items.len()`. On Enter, `activate_item(cursor, focus=true)` dispatches by `ItemTarget`:
  - `Todo(id)` → `open_document("todo", Some(id), true)` (spawns right-pane viewer via Zellij command).
  - `Scratchpad(id)` → `open_document("scratchpad", Some(id), true)`.
  - `Process(id)` / `Pane(pane)` → `route_pane_to_slot()` (swaps pane into a fixed slot, managing TTY visibility).
  - Focus argument controls whether keyboard moves to content pane (true) or stays on sidebar (false).

---

### 6. Talking to Daemon from Plugin
**CRITICAL:** The plugin does **NOT** call panopt MCP tools or HTTP endpoints from inside WASM. No `http`, `reqwest`, `tool_call`, or `web_request` code.

Instead:
- Plugin reads `.panopt/*.md` projection files from disk (line 991–996: `read_index()`, `read_processes()`; line 2430–2441 show file I/O).
- To invoke CLI commands (todo delete, agent spawn), plugin spawns child processes via Zellij's `open_command_pane_floating()` / `open_floating_pane()` (e.g., line 1625 for delete gate).
- **Pipes to/from daemon:** Plugin receives `PipeMessage` on named pipes like `"panopt:spawn-agent"` (line 647), `"panopt:delete-gate-decision"` (line 655), etc. — gatekeeping logic for cockpit-wide operations.

**What the plugin CAN do:**
- Read files from `/host/.panopt/` (projection mounts).
- Launch CLI commands as child processes (spawning viewers, agents, dialogs).
- Receive & respond to Zellij pipes.
- Manipulate pane focus/routing via Zellij host calls.

**Design implication:** A search popup cannot call `todo_search` / `scratchpad_search` MCP tools directly. It must either:
1. Read filtered projections from disk and filter in-pane (requires daemon to write a search-results file).
2. Spawn a child CLI command that calls MCP and reads results.

---

### 7. Plugin Reload / Refresh Trigger
**Lines 593–622** (`update()` on `Event::Timer`). Called every `1.0s` (line 620: `set_timeout(1.0)`):

- `self.reload_data()` (line 611): Re-reads `.panopt/todos.md`, `.panopt/scratchpads.md`, `.panopt/processes.md` from disk.
- `self.rebuild_items()` (line 612): Applies filter/sort and rebuilds the visible list.
- `self.sync_pane_titles()` (line 619): Updates right-pane titles (Todos mode only).

No filesystem watchers. No explicit sync. Pure timer-driven polling at 1 Hz. A pane manifest update (line 586: `Event::PaneUpdate`) also triggers `ingest_panes()` + `rebuild_items()`.

---

### 8. Bottom Indicator / Cursor Row
**Render reserves space by mode:**
- **Todos:** `list_rows()` returns `rows.saturating_sub(1)` (line 1536). Bottom row is always drawn as status line if `body_rows >= 2` (line 756). Shows sort axes, dim style.
- **Other modes:** `list_rows()` returns `rows` (full height). No reserved row.

Frame title handles scroll indicator: `({start}-{end}/{total})` appended to mode label if list overflows (lines 800–805).

**For a search query input line:** A dedicated Search mode could follow the Todos pattern: reserve the last row for input (like status line), or use a floating dialog pane spawned on `/` keypress. The render loop can position a cursor input line at `row == body_rows` with `\x1b[{body_rows};1H` and paint it separately.

---

**Summary:** Single-file WASM plugin, no HTTP. Five mode instances. Each mode reads projections + pane manifest once per second. Items rebuild from filtered/sorted projections every update. Render uses absolute ANSI cursor positioning. No text input anywhere—every interaction is single-key navigation + cursor movement. The Help overlay (?) shows full keymap. The `/` key is ready for a search/filter feature.

content.text:
Now I have enough context. Let me compile my findings:

## Investigation Report: Panopt Cockpit Plugin Architecture

### 1. **Spawning Plugin Panes & WASM Paths**

**File:** `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt/src/up.rs` (lines 28-94)

The cockpit layout template (`LAYOUT_TEMPLATE`) hard-codes five plugin instances stacked vertically on the left:
- `mode "todos"`, `mode "agents"`, `mode "terminals"`, `mode "commands"`, `mode "scratchpads"`

Each is configured identically with the same `.wasm` binary but distinct `mode` values. Zellij keys plugin identity on `(URL, configuration)`, so the five modes create five separate instances (line 14-17).

**WASM artifact path:** Resolved by `default_plugin_wasm()` (line 323-328):
```
<repo>/crates/panopt-zellij/target/wasm32-wasip1/release/panopt-zellij.wasm
```

**Template substitutions** (line 333-337): `{{PROJECT}}`, `{{WASM}}`, `{{PANOPT_BIN}}`, `{{PORT}}` are replaced at render time.

### 2. **Global Keybinds & Plugin Pane Routing**

**File:** `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/src/up.rs` (lines 96-139, 643-666)

**The pipe-based pattern:** Keybinds don't directly spawn plugin panes. Instead, they invoke `zellij action pipe --name "panopt:<action>" --plugin-configuration "mode=todos"` (line 119, 134, 136, 138). This pipes a message to the running plugin.

Key binds rewritten:
- `Alt-N` (NEW_PANE_BIND_FROM/TO, line 118-119): Retargets to `panopt:spawn-blank-pane` pipe sent to Todos pane only
- `x` (close focus/tab): Routes to `panopt:close-focus-request` or `panopt:close-tab-request`
- `Ctrl-Q` (quit): Routes to `panopt:quit-request`

**Why pipes?** (line 106-112): `MessagePlugin` keybinds include metadata that changes the plugin's configuration hash, causing Zellij to launch a *second* instance. Pipes go through the IPC bus and reach the existing plugin without that mismatch.

**Locked-mode keybinds** (line 643-666, `EXTRA_LOCKED_KEYBINDS`): Injected at render time to support Ctrl-S (scroll), PageUp/Down, Alt-arrow (pane navigation), Alt-N, Ctrl-Q in locked mode without requiring Ctrl-G escape first.

### 3. **Swap-In-Place: The Suppression Model**

**File:** `/home/gregz/cp/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs` (lines 1813-1829, 1126-1130, 1787-1820)

**Mechanism:**
- Each pane tracks a `suppressed: bool` flag (line 477, mirrored from Zellij's manifest via `is_suppressed`).
- The plugin maintains `slot_pane: Option<PaneId>` — the content pane currently visible on the right (line 370).
- When a sidebar item is selected (e.g., a todo), the plugin calls `show_in_slot(pane, focus)` (line 1813-1829):
  - If `slot_pane` exists (something is already showing), call `replace_pane_with_existing_pane(slot, pane, true)` — this hides the old pane and shows the new one.
  - If no slot yet, call `show_pane_with_id(pane, false, false)`.
  - The previous pane is suppressed (stays running hidden), not closed.

**Rehydration:** When there's no visible viewer, `first_suppressed_viewer()` (line 1126-1130) finds the most recent suppressed viewer and calls `show_in_slot()` to restore it.

**Host API calls** are from Zellij SDK (`zellij_tile::prelude::*`, line 32):
- `replace_pane_with_existing_pane(slot_id, pane_id, true)` — swap out, suppress old one
- `show_pane_with_id(pane_id, false, false)` — show hidden pane
- `focus_pane_with_id(pane_id, false, false)` — focus

### 4. **Right-Pane Terminal UX (Current Branch)**

**Recent commits** (`git log --oneline -10`):
- `ccb8975`: Restore auto-copy on drag release (clipboard UX)
- `755295b`: Stop auto-copying on drag release; Ctrl-Shift-C copies everywhere
- `faf2b8a`: Write OSC 52 to Zellij client's pty fd via `/proc` (major clipboard fix)
- Earlier: Drag-to-select, clipboard routing changes

**Modified files:**
- `crates/panopt/src/up.rs`: Expanded `copy_helper_script()` logic (line 372-447) to hunt for Zellij client's fd/1 in `/proc` and write OSC 52 directly to the user's terminal, bypassing Zellij's pre-VTE path (which fails on WezTerm-via-SSH).
- `crates/panopt-core/src/state.rs`, `crates/panopt-tool-surface/src/lib.rs`: Minor handler changes (not visible in diff yet — likely test/diagnostic updates).

### 5. **MCP from the Plugin: Two Paths**

**Path A (Plugin → Daemon via CLI shell-out):**

The plugin cannot directly call MCP tools. However, it can:
1. Shell out to `panopt <cmd>` (e.g., `panopt todo list`) via the viewer pane's command execution
2. The viewer runs `panopt _viewer` which itself connects to the daemon via the MCP proxy

**Path B (Agent/Claude Code path):**

Agents connect to panoptd via `panopt _mcp-proxy` (stdio MCP server):

**File:** `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/src/mcp_proxy.rs` (lines 1-80)
- Runs as a long-lived stdio MCP server spawned by Claude Code
- Maintains HTTP connection to panoptd with bearer-token auth
- Forwards `tools/list` and `tools/call` to panoptd over HTTP
- Automatically reconnects on daemon restart (transparent to Claude)

**File:** `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/src/agent_config.rs` (lines 29-77)
- Emits `--mcp-config` JSON for hand-launched agents
- Injects `panopt _mcp-proxy --host <host> --ws <ws> --id <id> --name <name> --token <token>` as the stdio command
- The proxy bakes the panopt binary's absolute path so it uses the same binary the user invoked

### 6. **Token Loading & Accessibility**

**File:** `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/src/paths.rs` (line 38-40)
```rust
pub fn token() -> Result<PathBuf> {
    Ok(data_dir()?.join("token"))
}
```

Location: `~/.local/share/panopt/token` (via `dirs::data_dir()`, line 17-22)

**Accessibility to the plugin:**
- The plugin **cannot directly read the token** — it's a Zellij WASM binary with limited filesystem access.
- **Solution:** The launcher (`panopt up`) writes the token into the environment of spawned panes (viewer, agent, etc.) as `PANOPT_TOKEN`.
- A pane can then use CLI commands that inherit `PANOPT_TOKEN` from the environment.

**File:** `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/src/agent_config.rs` (line 41-46) shows token reading for CLI use:
```rust
let token = match token {
    Some(t) => t,
    None => panopt_core::auth::read_token(&paths::token()?).context(...)?
};
```

---

**Summary for search popup design:** A new "search" plugin pane mode can follow the same pattern as the five existing ones — it receives `mode "search"` and `port "{{PORT}}"` in the layout. To trigger it on-demand with a keybind (e.g. `Ctrl-/`), add a new `bind` that pipes to a `panopt:open-search-pane` message routed to the gatekeeper (Todos pane). The search results would swap into the slot via the existing `show_in_slot()` flow, with no special MCP access needed — the plugin can route pane IDs and the viewer handles the data.

## Investigation Report: Panopt Cockpit Plugin Architecture

### 1. **Spawning Plugin Panes & WASM Paths**

**File:** `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt/src/up.rs` (lines 28-94)

The cockpit layout template (`LAYOUT_TEMPLATE`) hard-codes five plugin instances stacked vertically on the left:
- `mode "todos"`, `mode "agents"`, `mode "terminals"`, `mode "commands"`, `mode "scratchpads"`

Each is configured identically with the same `.wasm` binary but distinct `mode` values. Zellij keys plugin identity on `(URL, configuration)`, so the five modes create five separate instances (line 14-17).

**WASM artifact path:** Resolved by `default_plugin_wasm()` (line 323-328):
```
<repo>/crates/panopt-zellij/target/wasm32-wasip1/release/panopt-zellij.wasm
```

**Template substitutions** (line 333-337): `{{PROJECT}}`, `{{WASM}}`, `{{PANOPT_BIN}}`, `{{PORT}}` are replaced at render time.

### 2. **Global Keybinds & Plugin Pane Routing**

**File:** `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/src/up.rs` (lines 96-139, 643-666)

**The pipe-based pattern:** Keybinds don't directly spawn plugin panes. Instead, they invoke `zellij action pipe --name "panopt:<action>" --plugin-configuration "mode=todos"` (line 119, 134, 136, 138). This pipes a message to the running plugin.

Key binds rewritten:
- `Alt-N` (NEW_PANE_BIND_FROM/TO, line 118-119): Retargets to `panopt:spawn-blank-pane` pipe sent to Todos pane only
- `x` (close focus/tab): Routes to `panopt:close-focus-request` or `panopt:close-tab-request`
- `Ctrl-Q` (quit): Routes to `panopt:quit-request`

**Why pipes?** (line 106-112): `MessagePlugin` keybinds include metadata that changes the plugin's configuration hash, causing Zellij to launch a *second* instance. Pipes go through the IPC bus and reach the existing plugin without that mismatch.

**Locked-mode keybinds** (line 643-666, `EXTRA_LOCKED_KEYBINDS`): Injected at render time to support Ctrl-S (scroll), PageUp/Down, Alt-arrow (pane navigation), Alt-N, Ctrl-Q in locked mode without requiring Ctrl-G escape first.

### 3. **Swap-In-Place: The Suppression Model**

**File:** `/home/gregz/cp/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs` (lines 1813-1829, 1126-1130, 1787-1820)

- `focus_pane_with_id(pane_id, false, false)` — focus

I have what I need. Critical findings shaping the design:

- **The plugin has NO HTTP path** — it reads `.panopt/*.md` projections and shells out via Zellij command panes. It cannot call `todo_search` from WASM.
- **There's an established pipe-based pattern** for cockpit-wide actions: `zellij action pipe --name "panopt:foo"` → routed to the Todos plugin instance which handles it as a gatekeeper.
- **`PANOPT_TOKEN` is in the env of spawned panes**, so a child CLI process can authenticate to the daemon.
- **`open_floating_pane` is the existing mechanism** for transient interactive dialogs (delete gate, spawn dialog).

This shapes the design toward a **hybrid: plugin handles keybind + pane lifecycle, a tiny new `panopt search` CLI does the actual interactive search in the floating pane**, talking to the daemon via the same HTTP+token machinery `panopt _mcp-proxy` already uses.

Writing the plan now.

input.content:
# Plan: Dedicated search popup pane (#33 follow-up)

## Context

The MCP `todo_search` / `scratchpad_search` tools landed in the prior plan and now exist on the daemon. But the cockpit's only UI for finding things is scrolling the Todos sidebar — there's no body match, no cross-kind search, no jump-to-result. The user wants a popup pane: hit a keybind, type a query, see live results across todos and scratchpads, Enter to view, Esc to dismiss.

Two hard constraints from the codebase:

1. **The Zellij plugin cannot make HTTP calls from WASM.** It reads `.panopt/*.md` and shells out — no `reqwest`, no `rmcp` client.
2. **The cockpit's existing extension pattern is pipe-mediated:** a keybind invokes `zellij action pipe --name "panopt:<action>"` which routes to the Todos plugin instance as gatekeeper (see `up.rs:96–139` and the existing pipe handlers in `main.rs:639–660`).

So the popup is split: the **plugin owns the keybind, pane lifecycle, and result-dispatch**; a **new `panopt search` CLI runs inside the floating pane** as a thin TUI that calls the MCP tools. This keeps every UI surface in the cockpit pipe-driven and reuses the auth/HTTP path that `panopt _mcp-proxy` (`src/mcp_proxy.rs`) already maintains.

## End-to-end flow

```
[user presses Alt-/]
      ↓ (Zellij keybind)
[zellij action pipe --name "panopt:open-search" --plugin-configuration "mode=todos"]
      ↓
[Todos plugin instance receives PipeMessage]
      ↓ open_command_pane_floating("panopt", ["search"], env=PANOPT_TOKEN)
[floating pane appears, runs `panopt search`]
      ↓ (TUI loop)
[user types "auth"]                             ← per keystroke
      ↓ debounced 150ms
[`panopt search` fires todo_search + scratchpad_search via panoptd HTTP]
      ↓
[TUI redraws results table]
[user presses Enter on row]
      ↓ `zellij action pipe --name "panopt:show-result"
         --plugin-configuration "mode=todos" --payload '{"kind":"todo","id":17}'`
      ↓ `panopt search` exits (floating pane closes)
[Todos plugin receives pipe, calls existing open_document("todo", Some(17), true)]
[right-side viewer shows the todo, same as Enter in the sidebar]
```

The plugin never grows an MCP client. The CLI never grows pane-routing logic. Each piece does the thing its layer is good at.

## Pieces to build

### 1. New CLI subcommand `panopt search`

**Location:** `crates/panopt/src/search.rs` (new file), wired into `crates/panopt/src/main.rs` next to the existing subcommands.

**Crate deps:** `crossterm` for terminal input + alt-screen + raw mode. Do **not** pull in `ratatui` — the UI is one input line + ~20 result rows, hand-rolled ANSI is fine and matches the rest of the codebase's "no TUI framework" stance.

**Auth path:** Use the same code `mcp_proxy.rs` uses to talk to the daemon. Look at how it reads `PANOPT_TOKEN` (env first) → `paths::token()` (file fallback) and constructs the bearer-authenticated HTTP client; lift the small wrapper into a shared helper module, e.g. `crates/panopt/src/daemon_client.rs`. Both `mcp_proxy.rs` and `search.rs` then call it. Don't duplicate the client.

**Tool calls:** Single helper that fires both `todo_search` and `scratchpad_search` in parallel via `tokio::join!`, returns a merged `Vec<SearchRow>`. The row enum carries the kind so the renderer can prefix `T` vs `S` and drop kind-specific columns (status/priority/assignee for todos, blank for scratchpads).

**Debounce:** 150ms after the last keystroke before firing. If a new keystroke arrives during the in-flight request, drop the result (don't render stale). Standard pattern: `tokio::select!` between a `tokio::time::sleep` and a key event; only fire when sleep wins.

**TUI layout (rendered with ANSI directly):**
```
 ┌─ panopt search ─────────────────────────────────────┐
 │ > auth_                                              │
 │                                                      │
 │   T  #17  Wire up auth middleware       open  high   │
 │ ▶ T  #23  Document JWT rotation         open  med    │
 │   T  #41  Audit auth telemetry          backlog low  │
 │   S  #45  Auth design notes                          │
 │                                                      │
 │ ↑↓ select  ⏎ open  esc close            42ms · 4hits │
 └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Key handling: `Up`/`Down` move cursor (`PgUp`/`PgDn` jump 10). `Enter` emits the show-result pipe and exits. `Esc` or `Ctrl-C` exits with no pipe. Every other printable key edits the query buffer (backspace, left/right arrow in the input — keep it simple, no history).

**On Enter:** Shell out `zellij action pipe --name panopt:show-result --plugin-configuration mode=todos --payload <json>` with payload `{"kind":"todo"|"scratchpad","id":<u64>}`, then exit the process. Use `tokio::process::Command`.

### 2. Plugin: receive `panopt:open-search`, spawn the floating pane

**Location:** `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs`, in the existing `Event::PipeMessage` handler (around line 639) where pipes are routed.

Pattern to follow: the existing `panopt:spawn-blank-pane` handler at line 647-ish. Add a `"panopt:open-search"` arm that calls `open_command_pane_floating(self.panopt_bin.clone(), vec!["search".into()], env)` (or whatever the Zellij SDK signature is — see the existing delete-gate floating-pane spawn for the exact call). The env needs `PANOPT_TOKEN`, the daemon `PORT`, and `PANOPT_WS` (project root), all of which the plugin already has in its state (`self.ws`, `self.port`, etc.).

Only the Todos pane instance handles this pipe — same single-instance gatekeeper convention as the other `panopt:*` pipes. Idempotency: if a search floating pane is already up, ignore the pipe rather than stacking.

### 3. Plugin: receive `panopt:show-result`, dispatch to viewer

**Same file, same handler.** Parse the JSON payload, switch on `kind`, and call the existing `open_document("todo", Some(id), true)` or `open_document("scratchpad", Some(id), true)` — the same path that Enter on a sidebar row takes. No new viewer code; just reuse `activate_item` / `open_document`'s entry points.

### 4. Cockpit keybind

**Location:** `crates/panopt/src/up.rs`, in the keybind KDL block (around lines 96–139, and the `EXTRA_LOCKED_KEYBINDS` block at 643–666 for locked mode).

Add `Alt-/` in both blocks, bound to `MessagePlugin` / pipe with name `"panopt:open-search"` and `plugin-configuration "mode=todos"`. Follow the exact shape of the existing `Alt-N` → `panopt:spawn-blank-pane` rewrite for the unlocked path, and the existing pipe-style entries in `EXTRA_LOCKED_KEYBINDS` for the locked path. Both paths needed so the popup works whether the user is in normal mode or locked.

## Critical files

- `crates/panopt/src/search.rs` — **new**, the TUI
- `crates/panopt/src/daemon_client.rs` — **new**, lifted from `mcp_proxy.rs`'s HTTP wrapper
- `crates/panopt/src/mcp_proxy.rs` — refactored to use `daemon_client.rs`
- `crates/panopt/src/main.rs` — register the `search` subcommand
- `crates/panopt/src/up.rs` — add the keybind (both unlocked and locked KDL blocks)
- `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs` — pipe handlers for `panopt:open-search` and `panopt:show-result`
- `crates/panopt/Cargo.toml` — add `crossterm` dep

## Reuse / existing patterns

- **`mcp_proxy.rs`** already has the bearer-token HTTP client to the daemon — extract and share.
- **`activate_item` / `open_document`** in the plugin already swaps the right-side pane to a todo or scratchpad viewer — `panopt:show-result` calls into it.
- **`EXTRA_LOCKED_KEYBINDS` + the unlocked KDL block** in `up.rs` are the existing keybind extension points.
- **`open_command_pane_floating`** is already used by the delete-gate flow — the search popup is structurally identical (transient floating pane running a CLI).
- **Pipe-mediated extension** — every `panopt:*` action follows this pattern; the search pane is one more.

## Out of scope

- **Searching agents/processes/terminals.** Scope this to todos + scratchpads — those are the only kinds with `_search` MCP tools today. Add other kinds when their search tools exist.
- **Fuzzy / smart-case / multi-token query parsing.** Substring is what the MCP tool exposes; the TUI passes the query string straight through. fzf-style fuzzy can come later if needed.
- **Persistent search history.** The TUI starts with an empty query each time. (Easy follow-up via `~/.local/share/panopt/search_history`.)
- **In-place sidebar `/` filter.** Discussed earlier as the simpler alternative. Skipping per the user's preference for the popup. The two could coexist later if scrolling-and-narrowing turns out to be a separate gesture worth its own affordance.
- **Result preview pane.** No body preview inside the popup; Enter opens the viewer, which is the preview. Adds complexity without clear benefit.

## Verification

1. **Compile sweep:** `just check && just clippy` — exercises workspace + WASM plugin under `-D warnings`. `cargo build -p panopt --bin panopt` should produce the new CLI subcommand.
2. **Plugin reload:** `just plugin-release && just stop && just up` — `panopt up` reuses a running daemon, but Zellij needs the new plugin binary and the new keybind config, which means a full cockpit restart.
3. **Keybind path (unlocked):** With the cockpit up and focus anywhere except a plugin pane, press `Alt-/`. A floating pane should appear running `panopt search`.
4. **Keybind path (locked):** Press `Ctrl-G` to enter locked mode, then `Alt-/`. Same result — the locked-mode binding mirrors the unlocked one.
5. **Live search:** Type `search`. Within ~200ms the result list should include todo #33 (title `search todos`) and todo #46 (`have a universal object search`). Continue typing to refine; results re-render.
6. **Cross-kind:** Type a substring that matches both a todo title and a scratchpad title (e.g. `terminal`). Results should show both, with `T` / `S` prefixes.
7. **Selection → viewer:** `↓` to a result, `Enter`. Floating pane closes; the cockpit's right-side viewer should show that todo/scratchpad — same behavior as pressing Enter on the row in the sidebar.
8. **Esc:** Open the popup, type a query, press `Esc`. Floating pane closes, no pane swap, focus returns.
9. **Concurrent typing:** Type fast enough that multiple keystrokes land within 150ms. Only one MCP call should fire per debounce window (verify by tailing `~/.local/share/panopt/panoptd.log`).
10. **Auth failure path:** Temporarily unset `PANOPT_TOKEN` from the spawned env and confirm the TUI shows a clear "not authenticated" error in the result area rather than crashing.

## Open questions deferred to implementation

- **Exact crossterm signature for raw-mode + alt-screen on Zellij's pty.** Zellij's command panes already provide a clean pty; raw mode should just work, but worth confirming in a 10-line spike before fleshing out the TUI.
- **Whether the floating pane gets focus automatically when spawned.** Existing delete-gate flow does — if it doesn't, add the explicit `focus_pane_with_id` call.
- **Idempotency mechanism.** Track a `search_pane: Option<PaneId>` field on the plugin, clear it when the pane manifest shows it gone. Drop incoming `panopt:open-search` pipes when `search_pane.is_some()`.

Two hard constraints from the codebase:

1. **The Zellij plugin cannot make HTTP calls from WASM.** It reads `.panopt/*.md` and shells out — no `reqwest`, no `rmcp` client.
2. **The cockpit's existing extension pattern is pipe-mediated:** a keybind invokes `zellij action pipe --name "panopt:<action>"` which routes to the Todos plugin instance as gatekeeper (see `up.rs:96–139` and the existing pipe handlers in `main.rs:639–660`).

So the popup is split: the **plugin owns the keybind, pane lifecycle, and result-dispatch**; a **new `panopt search` CLI runs inside the floating pane** as a thin TUI that calls the MCP tools. This keeps every UI surface in the cockpit pipe-driven and reuses the auth/HTTP path that `panopt _mcp-proxy` (`src/mcp_proxy.rs`) already maintains.

## End-to-end flow

```
[user presses Alt-/]
      ↓ (Zellij keybind)
[zellij action pipe --name "panopt:open-search" --plugin-configuration "mode=todos"]
      ↓
[Todos plugin instance receives PipeMessage]
      ↓ open_command_pane_floating("panopt", ["search"], env=PANOPT_TOKEN)
[floating pane appears, runs `panopt search`]
      ↓ (TUI loop)
[user types "auth"]                             ← per keystroke
      ↓ debounced 150ms
[`panopt search` fires todo_search + scratchpad_search via panoptd HTTP]
      ↓
[TUI redraws results table]
[user presses Enter on row]
      ↓ `zellij action pipe --name "panopt:show-result"
         --plugin-configuration "mode=todos" --payload '{"kind":"todo","id":17}'`
      ↓ `panopt search` exits (floating pane closes)
[Todos plugin receives pipe, calls existing open_document("todo", Some(17), true)]
[right-side viewer shows the todo, same as Enter in the sidebar]
```

The plugin never grows an MCP client. The CLI never grows pane-routing logic. Each piece does the thing its layer is good at.

## Pieces to build

### 1. New CLI subcommand `panopt search`

**Location:** `crates/panopt/src/search.rs` (new file), wired into `crates/panopt/src/main.rs` next to the existing subcommands.

**Crate deps:** `crossterm` for terminal input + alt-screen + raw mode. Do **not** pull in `ratatui` — the UI is one input line + ~20 result rows, hand-rolled ANSI is fine and matches the rest of the codebase's "no TUI framework" stance.

**Auth path:** Use the same code `mcp_proxy.rs` uses to talk to the daemon. Look at how it reads `PANOPT_TOKEN` (env first) → `paths::token()` (file fallback) and constructs the bearer-authenticated HTTP client; lift the small wrapper into a shared helper module, e.g. `crates/panopt/src/daemon_client.rs`. Both `mcp_proxy.rs` and `search.rs` then call it. Don't duplicate the client.

**Tool calls:** Single helper that fires both `todo_search` and `scratchpad_search` in parallel via `tokio::join!`, returns a merged `Vec<SearchRow>`. The row enum carries the kind so the renderer can prefix `T` vs `S` and drop kind-specific columns (status/priority/assignee for todos, blank for scratchpads).

**Debounce:** 150ms after the last keystroke before firing. If a new keystroke arrives during the in-flight request, drop the result (don't render stale). Standard pattern: `tokio::select!` between a `tokio::time::sleep` and a key event; only fire when sleep wins.

**TUI layout (rendered with ANSI directly):**
```
 ┌─ panopt search ─────────────────────────────────────┐
 │ > auth_                                              │
 │                                                      │
 │   T  #17  Wire up auth middleware       open  high   │
 │ ▶ T  #23  Document JWT rotation         open  med    │
 │   T  #41  Audit auth telemetry          backlog low  │
 │   S  #45  Auth design notes                          │
 │                                                      │
 │ ↑↓ select  ⏎ open  esc close            42ms · 4hits │
 └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Key handling: `Up`/`Down` move cursor (`PgUp`/`PgDn` jump 10). `Enter` emits the show-result pipe and exits. `Esc` or `Ctrl-C` exits with no pipe. Every other printable key edits the query buffer (backspace, left/right arrow in the input — keep it simple, no history).

**On Enter:** Shell out `zellij action pipe --name panopt:show-result --plugin-configuration mode=todos --payload <json>` with payload `{"kind":"todo"|"scratchpad","id":<u64>}`, then exit the process. Use `tokio::process::Command`.

### 2. Plugin: receive `panopt:open-search`, spawn the floating pane

**Location:** `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs`, in the existing `Event::PipeMessage` handler (around line 639) where pipes are routed.

Pattern to follow: the existing `panopt:spawn-blank-pane` handler at line 647-ish. Add a `"panopt:open-search"` arm that calls `open_command_pane_floating(self.panopt_bin.clone(), vec!["search".into()], env)` (or whatever the Zellij SDK signature is — see the existing delete-gate floating-pane spawn for the exact call). The env needs `PANOPT_TOKEN`, the daemon `PORT`, and `PANOPT_WS` (project root), all of which the plugin already has in its state (`self.ws`, `self.port`, etc.).

Only the Todos pane instance handles this pipe — same single-instance gatekeeper convention as the other `panopt:*` pipes. Idempotency: if a search floating pane is already up, ignore the pipe rather than stacking.

### 3. Plugin: receive `panopt:show-result`, dispatch to viewer

**Same file, same handler.** Parse the JSON payload, switch on `kind`, and call the existing `open_document("todo", Some(id), true)` or `open_document("scratchpad", Some(id), true)` — the same path that Enter on a sidebar row takes. No new viewer code; just reuse `activate_item` / `open_document`'s entry points.

### 4. Cockpit keybind

**Location:** `crates/panopt/src/up.rs`, in the keybind KDL block (around lines 96–139, and the `EXTRA_LOCKED_KEYBINDS` block at 643–666 for locked mode).

Add `Alt-/` in both blocks, bound to `MessagePlugin` / pipe with name `"panopt:open-search"` and `plugin-configuration "mode=todos"`. Follow the exact shape of the existing `Alt-N` → `panopt:spawn-blank-pane` rewrite for the unlocked path, and the existing pipe-style entries in `EXTRA_LOCKED_KEYBINDS` for the locked path. Both paths needed so the popup works whether the user is in normal mode or locked.

## Critical files

- `crates/panopt/src/search.rs` — **new**, the TUI
- `crates/panopt/src/daemon_client.rs` — **new**, lifted from `mcp_proxy.rs`'s HTTP wrapper
- `crates/panopt/src/mcp_proxy.rs` — refactored to use `daemon_client.rs`
- `crates/panopt/src/main.rs` — register the `search` subcommand
- `crates/panopt/src/up.rs` — add the keybind (both unlocked and locked KDL blocks)
- `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs` — pipe handlers for `panopt:open-search` and `panopt:show-result`
- `crates/panopt/Cargo.toml` — add `crossterm` dep

## Reuse / existing patterns

- **`mcp_proxy.rs`** already has the bearer-token HTTP client to the daemon — extract and share.
- **`activate_item` / `open_document`** in the plugin already swaps the right-side pane to a todo or scratchpad viewer — `panopt:show-result` calls into it.
- **`EXTRA_LOCKED_KEYBINDS` + the unlocked KDL block** in `up.rs` are the existing keybind extension points.
- **`open_command_pane_floating`** is already used by the delete-gate flow — the search popup is structurally identical (transient floating pane running a CLI).
- **Pipe-mediated extension** — every `panopt:*` action follows this pattern; the search pane is one more.

## Out of scope

- **Searching agents/processes/terminals.** Scope this to todos + scratchpads — those are the only kinds with `_search` MCP tools today. Add other kinds when their search tools exist.
- **Fuzzy / smart-case / multi-token query parsing.** Substring is what the MCP tool exposes; the TUI passes the query string straight through. fzf-style fuzzy can come later if needed.
- **Persistent search history.** The TUI starts with an empty query each time. (Easy follow-up via `~/.local/share/panopt/search_history`.)
- **In-place sidebar `/` filter.** Discussed earlier as the simpler alternative. Skipping per the user's preference for the popup. The two could coexist later if scrolling-and-narrowing turns out to be a separate gesture worth its own affordance.
- **Result preview pane.** No body preview inside the popup; Enter opens the viewer, which is the preview. Adds complexity without clear benefit.

## Verification

1. **Compile sweep:** `just check && just clippy` — exercises workspace + WASM plugin under `-D warnings`. `cargo build -p panopt --bin panopt` should produce the new CLI subcommand.
2. **Plugin reload:** `just plugin-release && just stop && just up` — `panopt up` reuses a running daemon, but Zellij needs the new plugin binary and the new keybind config, which means a full cockpit restart.
3. **Keybind path (unlocked):** With the cockpit up and focus anywhere except a plugin pane, press `Alt-/`. A floating pane should appear running `panopt search`.
4. **Keybind path (locked):** Press `Ctrl-G` to enter locked mode, then `Alt-/`. Same result — the locked-mode binding mirrors the unlocked one.
5. **Live search:** Type `search`. Within ~200ms the result list should include todo #33 (title `search todos`) and todo #46 (`have a universal object search`). Continue typing to refine; results re-render.
6. **Cross-kind:** Type a substring that matches both a todo title and a scratchpad title (e.g. `terminal`). Results should show both, with `T` / `S` prefixes.
7. **Selection → viewer:** `↓` to a result, `Enter`. Floating pane closes; the cockpit's right-side viewer should show that todo/scratchpad — same behavior as pressing Enter on the row in the sidebar.
8. **Esc:** Open the popup, type a query, press `Esc`. Floating pane closes, no pane swap, focus returns.
9. **Concurrent typing:** Type fast enough that multiple keystrokes land within 150ms. Only one MCP call should fire per debounce window (verify by tailing `~/.local/share/panopt/panoptd.log`).
10. **Auth failure path:** Temporarily unset `PANOPT_TOKEN` from the spawned env and confirm the TUI shows a clear "not authenticated" error in the result area rather than crashing.

## Open questions deferred to implementation

- **Exact crossterm signature for raw-mode + alt-screen on Zellij's pty.** Zellij's command panes already provide a clean pty; raw mode should just work, but worth confirming in a 10-line spike before fleshing out the TUI.
- **Whether the floating pane gets focus automatically when spawned.** Existing delete-gate flow does — if it doesn't, add the explicit `focus_pane_with_id` call.
- **Idempotency mechanism.** Track a `search_pane: Option<PaneId>` field on the plugin, clear it when the pane manifest shows it gone. Drop incoming `panopt:open-search` pipes when `search_pane.is_some()`.

lines: +# Plan: Dedicated search popup pane (#33 follow-up)

-Todo #33 ("search todos") asks for a way to find todos — and per its body, "probably scratchpads, too" — by text and filters. Today both `todo_list` and `scratchpad_list` are no-arg fetch-everything tools, so any agent wanting "all open bug-tagged todos" has to pull the full table and filter client-side. As the project accumulates todos and scratchpads this becomes wasteful for agents and unworkable as a UX (e.g. the cockpit can't lazily query, it has to ship the whole list).

+The MCP `todo_search` / `scratchpad_search` tools landed in the prior plan and now exist on the daemon. But the cockpit's only UI for finding things is scrolling the Todos sidebar — there's no body match, no cross-kind search, no jump-to-result. The user wants a popup pane: hit a keybind, type a query, see live results across todos and scratchpads, Enter to view, Esc to dismiss.

-This plan adds two new MCP tools, `todo_search` and `scratchpad_search`, returning the same summary DTOs as the existing list tools but accepting optional text + structured filters. Sibling todo #46 ("universal object search") is the eventual cross-kind search; this MVP is the per-kind precursor and intentionally does not unify the two kinds yet.

-**Approach: parameterized SQL with `LIKE` for text matching, exact-match for enums/assignee, tags filtered in Rust after fetch.** No FTS5, no schema migration. Rationale: the project scale (typically <1000 todos/scratchpads per project) makes `LIKE` plenty fast, and adding the `fts5` rusqlite feature plus a virtual-table migration is a meaningful surface area that should wait until search performance actually bites. FTS5 stays a clean future swap because the tool signatures don't expose how matching is implemented.
